message.content:
Репозиторий: /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP (монорепо: server/ Node+TS+Express+Mongoose+OpenAI; client/ React+Vite — это админка).
Ревью изменений ветки feat/knowledge-bank поверх базы 6431bf3..HEAD (2 коммита: админ-UI банка + переменный размер теста и качество вопросов).
Сначала прочитай диапазон диффа:  git -C /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP diff 6431bf3 HEAD -- <относящиеся пути>
Затем читай окружающий код, чтобы понять контекст. Ищи РЕАЛЬНЫЕ дефекты корректности и интеграции, а не стиль.
Контекст фич:
 - Бэкенд KC/банк (questionBank.service.ts, ktp.controller.ts, ktp.routes.ts): новый read-эндпоинт listItems вопросов банка (admin); size-параметр assembleNodeTest (разрешено 5/10/15/20), size подмешан в реюз-хэш; generateForCoverage переписан на циклы раундов + разброс difficulty; contentHashOf теперь хеширует текст+варианты+правильный ответ.
 - Test.model.ts: валидатор questions.length сменён с ===10 на диапазон 1..120.
 - test.routes.ts/test.controller.ts: /tests/node-bank принимает size; /generate(+guest) валидируют questionCount.
 - client: ktp.api.ts (+методы KC/банка), ktp.types.ts (+типы), components/ktp/TopicKnowledgeBank.tsx (новый), KtpCatalogPage.tsx (врезка per-topic), гейтинг по role==='admin'.
Возвращай только обоснованные находки с file/location/severity/detail/suggestedFix.

Адверсариально ПРОВЕРЬ находку — попробуй её ОПРОВЕРГНУТЬ, прочитав реальный код. Если это не настоящий дефект (ложноположительное, или уже корректно обрабатывается), верни isReal=false. По умолчанию при сомнении — isReal=false.

Находка:
{
  "file": "server/src/services/ai.service.ts",
  "location": "generateEntBatchedTest (строки 322-353); generateTest ent-ветка (строки 77-84)",
  "severity": "medium",
  "title": "Ранее 'мёртвый' ent-батчинг (>10 вопросов) и трайл-блоки 20/40 теперь реально сохраняются — до этой ветки они валились на Test-валидаторе ===10",
  "detail": "generateEntBatchedTest и поддержка questionCount уже существовали в базе 6431bf3, НО валидатор Test.model требовал ровно 10 вопросов. Значит любой поток, генерящий !=10 вопросов, доходил до Test.create и падал с Mongoose ValidationError (→ errorHandler даёт 400 'Validation Error'). Это касалось: (1) ent + questionCount>10 (батчинг на 20..120); (2) трайл-флоу — TestStartPage.tsx (строка 168) всегда шлёт testProfile:'ent' и questionCount из плана 20/10/10/40 (trial.service MAIN_TRIAL_BLOCKS=20/10/10 и профильные блоки=40); guest-трайл идёт через generateTestGuest→findOrCreateTest→Test.create. До этой ветки сохранение 20- и 40-вопросных трайл-тестов было сломано на уровне модели. Теперь оно проходит. Это и есть 'ранее мёртвый код, активирующийся неожиданно': это желаемая разблокировка, но она расширяет фактически работающую матрицу размеров без отдельного теста именно на сохранение/грейдинг 20/40-вопросных трайл-тестов.",
  "suggestedFix": "Добавить интеграционный тест на полный трайл-поток (генерация → save Test с 20 и 40 вопросами → submit/грейдинг → trialTopicMastery) и на ent questionCount=120 (12 батчей). Подтвердить, что время генерации 12 последовательных батчей по 10 укладывается в таймауты HTTP/прокси (generateEntBatchedTest делает batches=total/10 последовательных LLM-вызовов; для 120 это 12 синхронных вызовов в одном запросе)."
}

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- figma:figma-code-connect: Creates and maintains Figma Code Connect template files that map Figma components to code snippets. Use when the user mentions Code Connect, Figma component mapping, design-to-code translation, or asks to create/update .figma.ts or .figma.js files.
- figma:figma-create-new-file: **MANDATORY prerequisite** — you MUST invoke this skill BEFORE every `create_new_file` tool call. NEVER call `create_new_file` directly without loading this skill first. Trigger whenever the user wants a new blank Figma file — a new design, FigJam, or Slides file — or when you need a fresh file before calling `use_figma`. Usage — /figma-create-new-file [editorType] [fileName] (e.g. /figma-create-new-file figjam My Whiteboard, /figma-create-new-file slides Q3 Review)
- figma:figma-generate-design: Use this skill alongside figma-use when the task involves translating an application page, view, or multi-section layout into Figma. Triggers: 'write to Figma', 'create in Figma from code', 'push page to Figma', 'take this app/page and build it in Figma', 'create a screen', 'build a landing page in Figma', 'update the Figma screen to match code', 'convert this modal/dialog/drawer/panel to Figma'. This is the preferred workflow skill whenever the user wants to build or update a full page, modal, dialog, drawer, sidebar, panel, or any composed multi-section view in Figma from code or a description. Discovers design system components, variables, and styles from Code Connect files, existing screens, and library search, then imports them and assembles views incrementally section-by-section using design system tokens instead of hardcoded values.
- figma:figma-generate-diagram: MANDATORY prerequisite — load this skill BEFORE every `generate_diagram` tool call. NEVER call `generate_diagram` directly without loading this skill first. Trigger whenever the user asks to create, generate, draw, render, sketch, or build a diagram — flowchart, architecture diagram, sequence diagram, ERD or entity-relationship diagram, state diagram or state machine, gantt chart, or timeline. Also trigger when the user mentions Mermaid syntax or wants a system architecture, decision tree, dependency graph, API call flow, auth handshake, schema, or pipeline visualized in FigJam. Routes to type-specific guidance, sets universal Mermaid constraints, and tells you when to use a different diagram type or skip the tool entirely (mindmaps, pie charts, class diagrams, etc.).
- figma:figma-generate-library: Build or update a professional-grade design system in Figma from a codebase. Use when the user wants to create variables/tokens, build component libraries, create individual components with proper variant sets and variable bindings, set up theming (light/dark modes), document foundations, or reconcile gaps between code and Figma. Also use when the user asks to create or generate any component in Figma — even a single one — since components require proper variable foundations, variant states, and design token bindings to be production-quality. This skill teaches WHAT to build and in WHAT ORDER — it complements the `figma-use` skill which teaches HOW to call the Plugin API. Both skills should be loaded together.
- figma:figma-swiftui: SwiftUI ↔ Figma translation. Use whenever the user mentions Swift, SwiftUI, iOS, iPhone, or iPad — in EITHER direction — translating a Figma design into SwiftUI (design → code), or pushing SwiftUI views / screens / tokens back into a Figma file (code → design). Triggers on phrases like 'implement this Figma design in SwiftUI', 'build this screen in Swift', 'push this SwiftUI view to Figma', 'mirror my Swift code in a Figma file', or whenever a Figma URL appears alongside `.swift` files / an `.xcodeproj`. Routes to a direction-specific reference doc; loads alongside `figma-use` for the code → design path.
- figma:figma-use: **MANDATORY prerequisite** — you MUST invoke this skill BEFORE every `use_figma` tool call. NEVER call `use_figma` directly without loading this skill first. Skipping it causes common, hard-to-debug failures. Trigger whenever the user wants to perform a write action or a unique read action that requires JavaScript execution in the Figma file context — e.g. create/edit/delete nodes, set up variables or tokens, build components and variants, modify auto-layout or fills, bind variables to properties, or inspect file structure programmatically.
- figma:figma-use-figjam: This skill helps agents use Figma's use_figma MCP tool in the FigJam context. Can be used alongside figma-use which has foundational context for using the use_figma tool.
- figma:figma-use-slides: This skill helps agents use Figma's use_figma MCP tool in the Slides context. Can be used alongside figma-use which has foundational context for using the use_figma tool.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
60	   */
61	  async generateTest(
62	    content: IContentForAI,
63	    _previousQuestions: string[] = [],
64	    testProfile: TestGenerationProfile = 'ent',
65	    options?: { questionCount?: number }
66	  ): Promise<IGeneratedTest> {
67	    const apiKey = process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY;
68	    if (!apiKey) {
69	      throw new Error('OPENAI_API_KEY is not defined. Set it to enable real test generation.');
70	    }
71	
72	    const profile: TestGenerationProfile = testProfile === 'regular' ? 'regular' : 'ent';
73	    const qc = options?.questionCount;
74	    if (profile === 'regular' && qc != null && (qc < 1 || qc > 50)) {
75	      throw new Error('Обычный тест: укажите от 1 до 50 вопросов.');
76	    }
77	    if (profile === 'ent' && qc != null) {
78	      if (qc < 10 || qc > 120 || qc % 10 !== 0) {
79	        throw new Error('Формат ЕНТ: от 10 до 120 вопросов, кратно 10.');
80	      }
81	      if (qc > 10) {
82	        return this.generateEntBatchedTest(content, _previousQuestions, qc);
83	      }
84	    }
85	
86	    const expectedN =
87	      profile === 'regular' ? Math.min(50, Math.max(1, qc ?? 10)) : 10;
88	
89	    const sourceContentHash = this.computeTestContentHash(content.text, profile, qc);
90	
91	    const topicsList = content.metadata.topics?.length
92	      ? `- relatedContent.topicTitle — точное название темы из списка: [${content.metadata.topics.join(', ')}]. Если вопрос не привязан к конкретной теме, опусти topicTitle.`
93	      : '';
94	
95	    const languageRule = content.metadata.contentLanguage?.trim()
96	      ? `- Язык полей questionText, всех вариантов/текстов заданий, aiExplanation и topicTitle: ${content.metadata.contentLanguage.trim()}. Не смешивай с другим языком.`
97	      : '- Язык вопросов, вариантов ответов и пояснений должен совпадать с языком приведённого учебного текста (например текст на казахском — весь вывод на казахском; на русском — на русском; на английском — на английском).';
98	
99	    const focusN = profile === 'regular' ? expectedN : 10;
100	    const topicFocusHint = content.metadata.topicFocus?.trim()
101	      ? `- ВАЖНО: Все ${focusN} вопросов должны быть строго по теме «${content.metadata.topicFocus.trim()}» и опираться ТОЛЬКО на приведённый учебный текст. Если материала по теме мало — сократи охват вопросов, но НЕ добавляй сведения извне.`
102	      : '';
103	
104	    // Анти-галлюцинации: жёсткое заземление на источник (применяется всегда).
105	    const groundingRule =
106	      '- Опирайся ИСКЛЮЧИТЕЛЬНО на приведённый ниже учебный текст. Запрещено добавлять факты, определения, формулы или примеры, которых нет в тексте. Каждый вопрос и его правильный ответ должны однозначно следовать из источника.';
107	
108	    const regularN = expectedN;
109	    const regularSpec = [
110	      `Сгенерируй ровно ${regularN} вопросов по учебному материалу — обычный тест (классический формат, не смешанный ЕНТ).`,
111	      'Все задания одного типа: один верный ответ из четырёх вариантов.',
112	      `Формат ответа: один JSON-объект вида {"questions":[ ... ровно ${regularN} элементов ... ]} без Markdown.`,
113	      '',
114	      'Каждый элемент массива questions:',
115	      '- questionType: всегда строка "single_choice"',
116	      '- questionText: формулировка задания',
117	      '- options: ровно 4 строки — варианты ответа',
118	      '- correctOption: одна строка, дословно совпадающая с одним из options',
119	      '- aiExplanation: кратко 1–2 предложения',
120	      '- relatedContent: { "pages": [числа], "topicTitle"?: "..." } — pages обязателен (можно приблизительно).',
121	      topicsList,
122	      '- Если в контенте есть формулы LaTeX ($...$ или $$...$$), сохраняй их в текстах.',
123	      _previousQuestions.length > 0 ? `- Избегай повторения смысла этих вопросов: ${_previousQuestions.join(' | ')}` : '',
124	      '',
125	      `КРИТИЧНО: массив questions ОБЯЗАТЕЛЬНО ровно из ${regularN} элементов. Не ${regularN - 1}, не ${
126	        regularN + 1
127	      } — ровно ${regularN}.`
128	    ].join('\n');
129	
130	    const entSpec = [
131	      'Ты — эксперт по подготовке к ЕНТ (Казахстан). Сгенерируй ровно 10 вопросов в форматах, приближённых к реальному ЕНТ.',
132	      'Формат ответа: один JSON-объект вида {"questions":[ ... ровно 10 элементов ... ]} без Markdown, без пояснений до или после JSON.',
133	      '',
134	      'РАСПРЕДЕЛЕНИЕ ПО ТИПАМ (строго по одному на каждый номер):',
135	      '1–3: single_choice — один верный ответ, 4–5 вариантов в поле options, correctOption — точная строка из options.',
136	      '4–5: multiple_choice — несколько верных, в options от 4 до 10 строк (по возможности не меньше 5), correctOptions — массив из 2–4 строк (каждая должна входить в options).',
137	      '6–7: matching_single — сопоставление «один к одному». matchingLeft и matchingRight: по 3–5 элементов { "id": "L1", "text": "..." }; id уникальны внутри колонки. correctMatching: объект { "L1": "R2", ... } — каждый ключ из matchingLeft, значение — id из matchingRight; каждый правый id используется ровно один раз.',
138	      '8: matching_multiple — один ко многим: matchingLeft 2–5 элементов, matchingRight 3–10. correctMatching: { "L1": ["R1","R3"], ... } — непустые массивы id из matchingRight.',
139	      '9: short_answer — короткий ответ (число или слово). Поля: acceptableAnswers: массив из 1–5 эквивалентных форм (например "4", "четыре"). Без options.',
140	      '10: text_input — развёрнутый ввод. Обязательно задай проверку: acceptableKeywords (3–6 ключевых слов/фраз) ИЛИ acceptableAnswers (2–4 эталонных фрагмента) ИЛИ referenceAnswer (краткий образцовый ответ). Без options.',
141	      '',
142	      'Общие поля каждого объекта вопроса:',
143	      '- questionType: одно из: single_choice | multiple_choice | matching_single | matching_multiple | short_answer | text_input',
144	      '- questionText: формулировка задания',
145	      '- aiExplanation: 1–2 предложения пояснения после ответа (обязательно у каждого вопроса; не пустая строка). Допустимо поле explanation как синоним — сервер примет, но лучше aiExplanation.',
146	      '- relatedContent: { "pages": [числа], "topicTitle"?: "..." } — pages обязателен (можно приблизительно).',
147	      topicsList,
148	      '- Если в контенте есть формулы LaTeX ($...$ или $$...$$), сохраняй их в текстах.',
149	      _previousQuestions.length > 0 ? `- Избегай повторения смысла этих вопросов: ${_previousQuestions.join(' | ')}` : '',
150	      '',
151	      'КРИТИЧНО: массив questions ОБЯЗАТЕЛЬНО ровно из 10 элементов (индексы 0–9). Не 9, не 11 — только 10.'
152	    ].join('\n');
153	
154	    const specBody = profile === 'regular' ? regularSpec : entSpec;
155	    const prompt = [
156	      specBody,
157	      '',
158	      'Требования:',
159	      groundingRule,
160	      languageRule,
161	      topicFocusHint,
162	      '',
163	      'Контент для генерации:',
164	      content.text
165	    ]
166	      .filter(Boolean)
167	      .join('\n');
168	
169	    const contentPreview = content.text.length > 800
170	      ? `${content.text.slice(0, 800)}...`
171	      : content.text;
172	
173	    const systemContent =
174	      profile === 'regular'
175	        ? `Ты составляешь обычные тесты по учебнику: ровно ${expectedN} вопросов с четырьмя вариантами и одним верным ответом. Ответь одним JSON-объектом с ключом questions; без Markdown и без текста вне JSON.`
176	        : 'Ты генерируешь проверочные задания по учебному контенту в форматах, приближённых к ЕНТ (Казахстан): один и несколько верных ответов, сопоставление, короткий и развёрнутый ответ. Ответь одним JSON-объектом с ключом questions — массив из ровно 10 элементов; без Markdown и без текста вне JSON.';
177	
178	    const max_tokens =
179	      profile === 'regular' ? Math.min(12000, 1500 + expectedN * 500) : 16000;

content.text:
The diff shows the only change to `ai.service.ts` in this branch is the prompt additions about distractors (lines 384-388). The `generateEntBatchedTest` function and `questionCount` support are NOT new in this branch — they pre-exist. Let me verify this is the case and examine the key claim: did the Test model validator (===10) actually block these flows before this branch?

Let me look at the batching function and verify the pre-existence claim.

input.description: Check if batching/questionCount appears in this branch's diff

content.text: Confirmed: `generateEntBatchedTest` and `questionCount` support do NOT appear in the diff for `ai.service.ts` — they pre-existed in base 6431bf3. The only `ai.service.ts` change is the prompt text. Now let me verify the core claim: did the `===10` validator actually block 20/40-question test saves before this branch? Let me check the validator in the base commit and check whether the trial/ent flows actually reach `Test.create`.

input.description: Find all questionCount usages on server

content.text: Confirmed: base validator was `=== 10`. Now let me trace the actual flows. Let me find where `questionCount` is used and how the trial/guest flows reach `Test.create`.

The trial blocks indeed specify questionCount 20/10/10 (main) and 40 (profile). Now the critical question: does the trial flow actually go through `Test.create` with these counts, and was the `===10` validator actually pre-existing AND in the active path? Let me trace the trial generation flow and the guest flow. The finding's strength depends on whether `generateTestGuest`/the trial path actually passes `questionCount` to `Test.create` and whether the batching path was actually reachable before.

content.text:
The timeout concern is speculative — there's no specific timeout config that would be exceeded, and this applies equally to the pre-existing batched-ent path (qc=120 was already callable for non-trial ent tests via the controller before this branch; only the final `Test.create` save was blocked). The 12-batch timeout concern is orthogonal to the validator change.

I now have a complete and decisive picture. Let me summarize my assessment.

**Verdict: isReal = false.**

My findings from reading the actual code:

1. **The factual premise is accurate.** In base `6431bf3`: the validator was `questions.length !== 10`; `generateEntBatchedTest`, the qc>10 branching, the controller `genOpts` plumbing, and the trial blocks (20/10/10 + 40) all pre-existed. The client trial flow sends `testProfile:'ent'` + `questionCount` (20/40) + `forTrial:true`. So before this branch, saving a 20- or 40-question trial test via `Test.create` would indeed have hit the `===10` validator and failed. The branch's validator change (1..120) genuinely unblocks this.

2. **But this is not a defect in the change under review.** The finding itself describes the unblocking as "желаемая разблокировка" (the intended purpose of the change). The validator change is internally consistent: max 120 covers the batched-ent max (120) and the trial counts (20/40). There is no correctness or integration bug introduced — the change does exactly what it's supposed to.

3. **The substance of the finding reduces to "missing test coverage," which is not a code defect.** Moreover, the server project has **no test infrastructure at all** (no test files, no `__tests__`, no `test` script in package.json), so "no dedicated test for 20/40 save/grade" is true of every path in the codebase and isn't actionable as a diff-level finding.

4. **The secondary timeout concern is speculative** — no timeout config exists that would be specifically violated, and the 12-sequential-batch behavior for qc=120 pre-existed independently of this validator change.

Per the instruction to default to isReal=false when the finding is not a genuine defect (it is a desired behavior change plus a test-coverage suggestion, not a correctness/integration bug), I'll report this as not real.

content.text:
I've completed the adversarial verification of this finding.

**Verdict: not a real defect (isReal=false, severity low).**

The finding's factual claims are accurate — I confirmed by reading base `6431bf3` that the `===10` validator coexisted with already-present batched-ent generation, controller `questionCount` plumbing, and 20/10/10+40 trial blocks, and that the client trial flow drives `Test.create` with 20/40 questions. So the new `1..120` validator genuinely unblocks those saves.

But it does not qualify as a defect in the changes under review because:
- The unblocking is the **intended purpose** of the commit, and the change is internally consistent (max 120 covers both the batched-ent ceiling and the trial counts). No correctness/integration bug is introduced.
- The actionable substance is just "add an integration test" — and the server has **no test infrastructure whatsoever** (no test files, no `__tests__`, no `test` script), so this is non-actionable and true of every code path.
- The 12-batch timeout concern is speculative and pre-dated this change.
